mappings.rationaleAI: The cell membrane and its nature (A.1), compartmentalization of the cell through organelles (A.2), and cellular processes including signaling and motility are central to vital processes at the molecular level.

Skeletal muscle contracts via actin-myosin sarcomeres, directly relevant to striated or skeletal muscle in higher animals (D.2), with fiber types differing in oxidative capacity and twitch characteristics.
Molecular biology investigates molecular structures underlying cellular activity relevant to cell membrane function (A) and the central dogma processes underlying nerve impulse transmission (C).
Proteins form structural components of cell membranes (A.1) and actin-myosin contractile systems in muscle (D), with transport carriers mediating membrane permeability.
Biochemistry underpins cell membrane structure and transport mechanisms (A) and bioelectricity in living systems (B).
Physiology spans cell membrane transport (A), nerve impulse generation and transmission (C), and muscle contraction mechanisms (D) at the molecular level.
Blood plasma involves osmotic movement of water across cell membranes (A.3) and solute transport through membranes via concentration gradients (A.4).
The heart generates rhythmic contractions via cardiac muscle (D.3), with sinoatrial node pacemaker cells propagating electrical impulses.
Anatomy of the neuron including structure (C.1) and muscle tissue organization including contractile systems (D) are relevant to molecular-level vital processes.
The eye converts light into electrochemical neural signals through photoreceptor cells, relevant to the nerve impulse including transmission through synapses (C).
